summary: Protocol mandates malformed-trailing-line tolerance in text only. The sole concrete consumer (stampede-monitor.sh) uses wc -l, which is incidentally tolerant but performs no JSON parsing. No line-by-line parse-and-skip logic exists anywhere. AI commanders receive the ledger paths via env vars and are expected to self-implement tolerance per prompt guidance, not hardcoded logic.

findings.issue: Tolerance rule is documentation only — no implementing code
findings.details: protocol.json rule 4 states 'Readers must tolerate absent files and malformed trailing lines.' This string is generated by stampede.sh and written into protocol.json as advisory text. No code in any script actually performs line-by-line JSONL parsing with error recovery.

findings.issue: stampede-monitor.sh uses wc -l for all collab JSONL ledgers — incidentally tolerant
findings.details: count_jsonl_lines() (lines 32-38) does wc -l on proposals.jsonl, reviews.jsonl, improvements.jsonl, consensus.jsonl, and broadcasts.jsonl. A malformed or partial trailing line is counted as +1 line but causes no parse error. This is de-facto tolerance by omission of any JSON parsing, not intentional robustness.

findings.issue: No JSONL consumer parses ledger content line-by-line with exception handling
findings.details: Across all target files, zero code paths read a collab JSONL ledger and iterate lines with json.loads() + try/except. The monitor only counts lines. AI commanders receive ledger paths as env vars (stampede.sh:932-936) and are told via prompt text to consume broadcasts.jsonl, but no code enforces or implements skip-bad-line semantics.

findings.issue: swarm-state.json readers use json.load() with stderr suppressed — silent failure on corrupt file
findings.details: stampede-monitor.sh reads swarm-state.json via json.load() inside a heredoc Python block run with 2>/dev/null (lines 147-190). A malformed swarm-state.json silently yields an empty nested string rather than a crash. This is fault-tolerant at the shell level but semantically unhandled.

findings.issue: stampede-commander.agent.md delegates all JSONL read/write tolerance to AI behavior
findings.details: The agent spec instructs commanders to append to child-agents.jsonl and read broadcasts.jsonl but provides no error-handling pseudocode or skip-bad-line instruction. Tolerance depends entirely on the LLM's autonomous judgment.

findings.issue: SKILL.md references collab workflow only in prose; no JSONL reader code present
findings.details: skills/SKILL.md mentions the collab workflow (propose->peer_review->improve->consensus->broadcast->adopt) at line 407 and references orchestrator.jsonl at line 445 but contains no code that reads collab JSONL ledgers with any tolerance handling.
